text.title: Agent Eval Cost Budget Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-eval-cost-budget-policy enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Nightly cadence runs the full suite; per-PR cadence runs a fast subset ≤20% of full suite by cost.
rule.rationale: Full suite per PR is unaffordable; ≤20% subset catches the common regressions while keeping PR feedback under 10 minutes.

rule.statement: Fast subset is stratified-sampled across capability tags; not random.
rule.rationale: Random sampling over-represents common cases; stratified captures rare-but-load-bearing categories.

rule.statement: Weekly adversarial cadence runs red-team / jailbreak / OOD prompts; separate budget line.
rule.rationale: Mixing adversarial in nightly inflates baseline cost and hides regressions; weekly slot makes the spend visible.

rule.statement: Judge-model fallback: when day's eval spend exceeds 80% of ceiling, switch to a cheaper judge (e.g., Haiku/distilled) until ceiling resets; flag the swap in the report.
rule.rationale: Hard ceiling without fallback halts all evals; cheap fallback degrades signal gracefully.

rule.statement: Policy is versioned at `evals/policy.md` with date + signature; changes require approval.
rule.rationale: Drifting policies make eval cost trends impossible to read.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
